summary: Present queued MCP mutations in the History rail and require explicit in-app approval before dispatching external file or parameter changes.

decisions.decision: Permit mutation requests through the stdio dispatcher solely so the workbench handler can enqueue them; do not mutate at dispatch time. Make the review queue visible in History and perform the approved work through an external-agent command-bus dispatch.
decisions.rationale: A transport-level deny response gives no actionable review item, while direct handler mutation would bypass user review. This boundary preserves explicit approval, standard history records, and the existing render/undo behavior.

decisions.decision: Add a validated set-values parameter action so one approved MCP parameter request produces one runtime command and one external-agent history entry.
decisions.rationale: Applying values independently could create partial state and misleading multiple history records. The reducer retains only compatible visible Customizer values, as its existing batch-set semantics require.

testEvidence.criterion: Queued external file and parameter requests remain inert until explicit review, then flow through the command bus with an external-agent history record.
testEvidence.test: Run focused MCP review, stdio, parameter reducer, runtime, and theme-contract tests; run lint/build; then run one aggregate suite after the identified CSS token correction.

red.outcome: RED: 175 files/1274 tests passed, but the Appendix C CSS contract rejected the newly added nonexistent --chrome-error token. Replaced it with the allowed --chrome-badge-error token.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/application/theme/theme-css-variable-usage.test.ts tests/ui/mcp/McpReviewPanel.test.tsx --reporter=dot; pnpm.cmd test -- --reporter=dot
green.outcome: GREEN: targeted theme/review coverage passed 2 files and 4 tests; the one final aggregate passed 176 files and 1275 tests in 20.37 seconds. The earlier focused 5-file/33-test run, lint/source policy, and TypeScript/Vite build were also green.

nearMisses.sought: A pending-review response that also creates an actionable in-app review item
nearMisses.confirmation: The default mutation permission was deny, which returned pending_review before the handler could queue the proposal. The stdio hook explicitly allows only those requests into the handler, which still queues rather than mutates.

questions: Direct viewport screenshot capture, a real desktop subprocess walkthrough, and hosted AC-11.b/c evidence remain pending. The session-local default-off Settings toggle remains intentional.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
